tasks.title: Collapse registry: rename strategy→prompt_strategy in workflows + remove simple_rag_* builders
acceptance_criteria: SimpleRAGWorkflow.__init__ parameter renamed from 'strategy' to 'prompt_strategy'
CRAGWorkflow.__init__ parameter renamed from 'strategy' to 'prompt_strategy'
SummarizeRAGWorkflow.__init__ parameter renamed from 'strategy' to 'prompt_strategy'
CompositeWorkflows (crag_summarize, crag_review) propagate prompt_strategy
Registry reduced from 9 to 7 entries: simple_rag, crag, react, summarize_rag, crag_summarize, crag_review, react_review
_build_simple_rag_zero/few/cot removed; _build_simple_rag accepts prompt_strategy kwarg
get_workflow() accepts prompt_strategy kwarg and forwards it
python -m harness.workflows.registry --list shows exactly the 7 new entries
pytest tests/ passes

tasks.title: Update run_eval.py + app.py to read orchestration.prompt_strategy from YAML
acceptance_criteria: run_eval.py reads orch_cfg.get('prompt_strategy', 'zero_shot') and passes to get_workflow()
app.py builds workflow with prompt_strategy derived from ChatProfile selection
No reference to simple_rag_zero/few/cot registry keys anywhere in Python code
run_eval.py smoke test on ablation_c_mid_zero.yaml succeeds

tasks.title: Rewrite 10 YAML configs to use orchestration.strategy: simple_rag + prompt_strategy field
acceptance_criteria: All 9 ablation_c configs have orchestration.strategy: simple_rag + orchestration.prompt_strategy: zero_shot/few_shot/cot_self
workflow_simple_rag.yaml has orchestration.strategy: simple_rag + orchestration.prompt_strategy: zero_shot
No YAML file references simple_rag_zero, simple_rag_few, or simple_rag_cot
DECISIONS.md updated with registry collapse entry
All configs run without ValueError from get_workflow()

tasks.title: Add config_attributes() method to all 6 workflow classes
acceptance_criteria: SimpleRAGWorkflow.config_attributes() returns ema.orchestration.strategy='simple_rag', ema.orchestration.prompt_strategy, ema.retrieval.strategy, ema.retrieval.mode, ema.retrieval.k
CRAGWorkflow.config_attributes() returns above + ema.crag.max_cycles
ReActNativeWorkflow.config_attributes() returns ema.orchestration.strategy='react', ema.retrieval.* fields
SummarizeRAGWorkflow.config_attributes() returns its own orchestration strategy key
Composite workflows (crag_summarize, crag_review, react_review) return outer+inner keys
All return dict[str, str | int | float | bool]
All keys use ema.orchestration.*, ema.retrieval.*, ema.llm.* namespaces

tasks.title: Modify WorkflowRunner.ainvoke to stamp config_attributes() onto current OTel span
acceptance_criteria: WorkflowRunner.ainvoke reads config_attributes() from wrapped workflow if method exists
Attributes stamped onto opentelemetry.trace.get_current_span() before delegating to self._wf.run()
If current span is non-recording (Phoenix disabled), stamping is a silent no-op
If config_attributes() is absent on a workflow, logs a warning once and continues
No AttributeError, no RuntimeError when PHOENIX_DISABLED=1
ema.run.source stamped from inputs dict if present

tasks.title: Pass run_id + source tag through workflow inputs in app.py and run_eval.py
acceptance_criteria: app.py ainvoke call passes run_id=run_id and source='chainlit' in inputs dict
run_eval.py ainvoke/invoke call passes run_id=cfg['run_id'] and source='eval' in inputs dict
WorkflowRunner stamps ema.run.id and ema.run.source from these inputs
Existing thumbs-up/down feedback flow in app.py continues unchanged
Existing retrieval-only runs (no orchestration) are unaffected

tasks.title: Add pytest test: verify config_attributes() are stamped on root span
acceptance_criteria: Test mocks opentelemetry.trace.get_current_span() to return a recording span mock
Runs WorkflowRunner.ainvoke with a minimal SimpleRAGWorkflow (mocked LLM + index)
Asserts set_attribute called with ema.orchestration.strategy='simple_rag'
Asserts set_attribute called with ema.retrieval.mode='dense'
Second test sets PHOENIX_DISABLED=1 (non-recording span) — asserts no exception raised
Third test: workflow without config_attributes() — asserts warning logged, no crash
All three tests pass with pytest tests/test_span_attributes.py

tasks.title: Define AblationConfig dataclass + build_retrieve_fn factory in harness/retrieve.py
acceptance_criteria: AblationConfig dataclass with fields: query_expansion (dict), topic_filter (dict), reranker (str|None), reranker_max_chunks (int)
AblationConfig.from_yaml(abl_dict) classmethod parses the ablation: YAML section
build_retrieve_fn(ret_config, abl_config, index, hier_index=None) returns Callable[[str], list]
Returned callable applies A1 → base retrieval → A2 → A3/A4 in order (matching existing run_eval.py closure exactly)
Returned callable has .ablation_config attribute pointing to the AblationConfig
BM25 retriever caching in retrieve.py still functions (cache key still hits)
AblationConfig() with all defaults produces same results as no ablation (A0 baseline)

tasks.title: Thread retrieve_fn into all workflow __init__ methods with fallback
acceptance_criteria: Each workflow __init__ accepts optional retrieve_fn: Callable[[str], list] | None = None
When retrieve_fn is provided, retrieve step calls retrieve_fn(question) instead of retrieve_with_config()
When retrieve_fn is None, workflow falls back to existing retrieve_with_config() behaviour
Existing tests remain green (fallback path unchanged)
retrieve_fn is stored as self._retrieve_fn and used in all retrieve steps

tasks.title: Update registry get_workflow() + run_eval.py + app.py to build and pass retrieve_fn
acceptance_criteria: registry.get_workflow() accepts and forwards retrieve_fn kwarg
run_eval.py replaces inline retrieve_fn closure with build_retrieve_fn(ret_config, abl_config, index) call
Same retrieve_fn passed to both retrieval eval loop and get_workflow() in run_eval.py
app.py calls build_retrieve_fn once at session start (on_chat_start) with default AblationConfig
A YAML config with orchestration.strategy=crag + ablation.reranker=sme runs end-to-end
Existing A0-A5 ablation configs produce same retrieval metrics as before (within tolerance)

tasks.title: Expose AblationConfig in config_attributes() + update TRACE-007 test
acceptance_criteria: When retrieve_fn has .ablation_config attribute, config_attributes() reads it for ema.retrieval.reranker, ema.retrieval.query_expansion, ema.retrieval.topic_filter
When retrieve_fn is None or lacks .ablation_config, config_attributes() uses RetrievalConfig defaults
Phoenix span for CRAG + sme reranker shows ema.retrieval.reranker='sme'
Test in test_span_attributes.py updated to assert ablation attributes on span

tasks.title: Update docs/RETRIEVAL_PIPELINE.md + DECISIONS.md for Changes 1-3
acceptance_criteria: RETRIEVAL_PIPELINE.md 'Ablation stack' section updated to describe build_retrieve_fn factory and AblationConfig
RETRIEVAL_PIPELINE.md documents that retrieve_fn is shared across app.py and run_eval.py
DECISIONS.md entry for Change 1: Phoenix span attributes strategy and ema.* namespace choice
DECISIONS.md entry for Change 2: retrieve_fn factory replacing inline closure in run_eval.py
DECISIONS.md entry for Change 3: registry collapse and prompt_strategy YAML field

change_3.description: Collapse simple_rag_* registry entries; prompt_strategy as YAML field

change_1.description: Stamp configuration onto Phoenix span attributes

change_2.description: Push retrieval ablations into shared build_retrieve_fn factory
